intro: CUNY's origins go back to 1847, when the Free Academy — the nation's first free public institution of higher education — opened so that, in founder Townsend Harris's words, "the children of the rich and the poor take their seats together." The City University of New York was formally established by state legislation in 1961, uniting the city's existing municipal colleges into a single system. CUNY today comprises 26 colleges and professional schools across the five boroughs, serving roughly 247,000 degree-seeking students and remaining a primary engine of upward mobility for New Yorkers.
